title: linaria repens
intro: Linaria repens, also known as creeping toadflax or butter and eggs, is a perennial flowering plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its yellow, trumpet-shaped flowers and creeping stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Linaria repens include its ability to soothe digestive issues, calm anxiety and insomnia, and alleviate respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, Linaria repens is often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
